metadata.split_role: Expanded long-stemmed, peppery, chalciporoid, and early oddball bolete records; first half of former mushrooms-boletes-other.json.

records.culinary_uses: Not recommended as a food target in this guide; retained as a specialist comparison among small red-pored boletes.

mushroom_profile.summary: Small red-pored Chalciporus with yellow flesh and pinkish-red pores; type material was collected in Michigan, but use a conservative ID standard.
mushroom_profile.season_note: July to September in reported North American material.
research_notes: Small yellow to red-pink cap, red-pink pores fading yellow-orange/brown, yellow flesh that does not blue, and yellow-based pinkish stem.
Food role downgraded pending stronger non-Wikipedia source support.

records.edibility_detail: Specialist-ID comparison only. Do not use this app record as a food recommendation unless a stronger edible-use source and local confidence are added later.
records.mushroom_card_note: Small red-pored Chalciporus with yellow flesh and pinkish-red pores; type material was collected in Michigan, but use a conservative ID standard.
records.field_identification: Look for a small yellow to red-pink cap, red-pink pores fading yellow-orange/brown, yellow flesh that does not blue, and a yellow-based pinkish stem. Best treated as a specialist Chalciporus ID.
records.edibility_notes: Treat as specialist-ID only. Confirm the small yellow to red-pink cap, red-pink pores fading orange-brown, yellow flesh that does not blue, and yellow-based pinkish stem;

images_structured.title: Chalciporus Pseudorubinellus field view
images_structured.author: Eva Skific / Evica

edible_use.notes: Safety downgraded pending stronger non-Wikipedia source support.

records.image_rights_notes: Single exact-species Wikimedia Commons / Mushroom Observer image rights normalized in v4.3.150; record remains review-needed because non-Wikipedia species source support is still weak.
records.source_note: Image rights cleaned, but species/source review remains open; do not clear review without stronger non-Wikipedia support. v4.3.211 records the nomenclatural blocker and deliberately leaves spore and host claims unchanged.
records.taxonomy_note: Original Michigan taxon retained as an unresolved specialist comparison pending direct protologue and modern nomenclatural reconciliation.

records.culinary_uses: Comparison species rather than a main food mushroom.

mushroom_profile.summary: Small purple-red/red-pored bolete; useful as a comparison species among peppery/red-pored Chalciporus rather than a major edible.
research_notes: Small convex cap often purple-red to reddish brown, red to pinkish pore surface, yellow flesh that generally does not blue, and a bare stem. Compare with C. pseudorubinellus and C. piperatus.

records.edibility_detail: Use caution with small red-pored boletes. Confirm red to pinkish pores, yellow flesh that generally does not blue, and separation from C. pseudorubinellus and C. piperatus.
records.mushroom_card_note: Small purple-red/red-pored bolete; useful as a comparison species among peppery/red-pored Chalciporus rather than a major edible.
records.field_identification: Small convex cap often purple-red to reddish brown, red to pinkish pore surface, yellow flesh that generally does not blue, and a bare stem. Compare with C. pseudorubinellus and C. piperatus.
records.edibility_notes: Use caution with small red-pored boletes. Confirm red to pinkish pores, yellow flesh that generally does not blue, and separation from C. pseudorubinellus and C. piperatus.

edible_use.notes: Comparison/ID record only; not presented as a food target in this guide.

records.image_rights_notes: Two Chalciporus rubinellus images restored in v4.3.159-visible-debt-cleanup1 with exact Wikimedia Commons / Mushroom Observer CC BY-SA 3.0 metadata. Earlier v4.3.158 image clearing is superseded by this verification pass.
records.source_note: Verified Chalciporus rubinellus images restored in v4.3.159-visible-debt-cleanup1 after exact Commons file-page rights verification. Species/source review remains open because strong non-Wikipedia profile support still needs supervisor verification; keep as ID/comparison only. v4.3.211 records that likely conifer ecology and a brown print remain uninstalled until the exact Baroni or MycoQuébec species passage is directly verified.

use_links.label: NAMA: Mushrooms to Dye For
use_links.notes: Mentions Chalciporus rubinellus in dye context only; does not support edible use or field ID.

records.culinary_uses: Generally treated as edible but not usually a top-tier table mushroom; larvae often ruin mature stems.

mushroom_profile.summary: Pretty and distinctive bolete with pinkish cap, pink scabers on the stem, and a chrome-yellow stem base.
mushroom_profile.spore_print: Pinkish brown to cinnamon or purplish brown.
research_notes: The key combination is pinkish to tan cap, creamy pores becoming pinkish/brownish, pinkish scabers on the stem, and a bright chrome-yellow stem base/basal mycelium. Often formerly listed as Tylopilus or Leccinum chromapes.

records.edibility_detail: Edible with correct ID and fresh condition, but not prime.
records.mushroom_card_note: Pretty and distinctive bolete with pinkish cap, pink scabers on the stem, and a chrome-yellow stem base.
records.field_identification: The key combination is pinkish to tan cap, creamy pores becoming pinkish/brownish, pinkish scabers on the stem, and a bright chrome-yellow stem base/basal mycelium. Often formerly listed as Tylopilus or Leccinum chromapes.

images_structured.rights_notes: Exact author/license metadata verified from the Wikimedia Commons source page; unverified extra images were dropped from visible image arrays in v4.3.157.

records.image_rights_audit_note: Dropped unverified 655913 and 904696 images; exact source-page author/license verification was not available in the worker pass.
records.source_note: v4.3.210 adds the source-backed macro spore-print description and normalized color filters; spore color alone is not proof of species identity or edibility.

records.culinary_uses: ID/comparison record only in this guide; not presented as a food target.

mushroom_profile.summary: Clustered brown bolete with a dry brown cap, bright yellow non-bruising pores, yellow basal mycelium, and a stem often swollen above a short root; treated here as an ID/comparison record.
research_notes: Look for clustered growth, dry brown to reddish-brown cap, bright yellow pores that do not bruise blue, yellow basal mycelium, and a stem swollen near the base before tapering into a short root.
MushroomExpert supports the Boletus innixus concept but treats the Aureoboletus placement cautiously; keep the app record taxonomy-cautious and non-food-forward.

records.general_notes: Image coverage is currently suppressed pending exact rights verification. MushroomExpert supports the Boletus innixus concept; the Aureoboletus placement should be treated as taxonomy-caution rather than food-use support.
records.edibility_detail: Use as an ID/comparison species only. MushroomExpert supports the Boletus innixus concept but does not provide edible-use support; do not present as a normal food target.
records.mushroom_card_note: Clustered brown bolete with a dry brown cap, bright yellow non-bruising pores, yellow basal mycelium, and a stem often swollen above a short root.
records.field_identification: Look for clustered growth, dry brown to reddish-brown cap, bright yellow pores that do not bruise blue, yellow basal mycelium, and a stem swollen near the base before tapering into a short root.
records.image_rights_audit_note: v4.3.160 status polish: no verified open-license image is currently available; record remains visible as imageless ID/comparison.
records.edibility_notes: Use as an ID/comparison species only. MushroomExpert supports the Boletus innixus concept but does not provide edible-use support; do not present as a normal food target.
records.source_note: MushroomExpert supports the Boletus innixus species concept for ID/comparison, but notes that the Aureoboletus innixus placement is not strongly supported there; food-role downgraded in v4.3.159-visible-debt-cleanup1. v4.3.210 adds a limited group-level spore-print description and normalized filters; finer separation remains review-sensitive and may require a thick print or microscopy.

edible_use.notes: ID/comparison record only; no strong edible-use source is present in the active record.

use_links.label: MushroomExpert: Boletus innixus
use_links.notes: Supports the Boletus innixus concept and provides taxonomy caution around the Aureoboletus placement; does not support edible-use promotion.
